segments.title: Bruships 248 question intake reopens during the live stream
segments.summary: The transcript shows the host reopening questions and waiting for the on-screen banner before continuing intake.
segments.body: The stream opens with audience-question setup. Bruships 248 continues into audience prompts after this setup.

evidence.note: Transcript says questions are open and references waiting for the banner before proceeding.

segments.title: 23-knot British battle line question
segments.summary: An audience question asks how Germany would respond if the Orion and King George V battleships matched 23 knots.
segments.body: The answer expects larger and more compromised German responses as the British battle line speed rises.

evidence.note: An audience question asks how Germany would respond if the Orion and King George V battleships matched 23 knots.

segments.question: If the Orion class and following King George V matched 23 knots, how would Germany respond?
segments.answerShort: Likely by building larger ships and making harder design sacrifices to keep up with the faster British battle line.

segments.title: Fire-control data between ships question
segments.summary: A Q&A window explains how ships could share fire-control information when one ship had systems shut down.
segments.body: The answer points to fire-control clocks and displayed data rather than assuming a modern networked system.

evidence.note: A Q&A window explains how ships could share fire-control information when one ship had systems shut down.

segments.question: How can one ship transmit fire-control data to another if that ship has its own system shut out?
segments.answerShort: Use fire-control clock and display arrangements to pass data between ships without relying on the disabled local system.

segments.title: Landlocked country with blue-water warships question
segments.summary: The stream answers whether any landlocked country currently has blue-water warships.
segments.body: The answer is essentially no, with the question treated as a quick force-structure edge case.

evidence.note: The stream answers whether any landlocked country currently has blue-water warships.

segments.question: Is there any landlocked country today with blue-water warships?
segments.answerShort: Probably not; the answer treats the idea as not meaningfully present today.

segments.title: Yorktown, Shokaku, or Illustrious carrier choice
segments.summary: A question asks which carrier class the panel would choose among Yorktown, Shokaku, and Illustrious.
segments.body: The answer depends on theatre, with Illustrious favored in the Mediterranean context.

evidence.note: A question asks which carrier class the panel would choose among Yorktown, Shokaku, and Illustrious.

segments.question: If you had to be on a Yorktown, Shokaku, or Illustrious class carrier, which one would you pick?
segments.answerShort: It depends on theatre; the answer favors Illustrious in the Mediterranean and treats context as decisive.

segments.title: Defence Investment Plan deficiency correction question
segments.summary: A question asks how long it would take to correct Defence Investment Plan deficiencies and rebuild required defence levels.
segments.body: The answer turns on whether the plan survives government changes and whether funding is actually delivered.

evidence.note: A question asks how long it would take to correct Defence Investment Plan deficiencies and rebuild required defence levels.

segments.question: How long would it take to correct deficiencies in the Defence Investment Plan and rebuild defence to required levels?
segments.answerShort: It depends on implementation, future governments, and real funding; the answer rejects a quick fix.

segments.title: Grace Dieu raising question
segments.summary: A viewer asks whether enough of Grace Dieu survives to make raising her worthwhile.
segments.body: The answer is yes from a naval historian perspective, with the caveat that marine archaeology expertise matters.

evidence.note: A viewer asks whether enough of Grace Dieu survives to make raising her worthwhile.

segments.question: Does enough of Grace Dieu survive to make raising her worthwhile?
segments.answerShort: Probably yes from a naval historian perspective, though the answer caveats that marine archaeology expertise is needed.

segments.title: World War I dreadnought threat to a World War II KGV
segments.summary: A question asks how many German World War I dreadnoughts would seriously threaten a World War II King George V.
segments.body: The answer notes that if older heavy guns get close enough, hits can still be a serious event.

evidence.note: A question asks how many German World War I dreadnoughts would seriously threaten a World War II King George V.

segments.question: How many World War I German dreadnoughts would be a significant threat to a World War II KGV?
segments.answerShort: At close enough range, even older heavy guns remain dangerous, so the threat cannot be dismissed purely by age.

segments.title: Mosquito-mounted bouncing bomb response question
segments.summary: A Q&A segment considers Axis responses if Britain used a Mosquito-mounted bouncing bomb against German battleships.
segments.body: The answer depends heavily on whether the British system works and achieves success.

evidence.note: A Q&A segment considers Axis responses if Britain used a Mosquito-mounted bouncing bomb against German battleships.

segments.question: What response would come from the Axis if Britain fielded a Mosquito-mounted bouncing bomb for German battleships?
segments.answerShort: The response would depend on demonstrated British success; a working system would force countermeasures and reactions.
